free_text: The Californium-252 Fission Neutron Spectrum from 0.5
to 13 MeV.
Time-of-flight, over 37 m.flight path.
Pulse-height and shape discrimination
Experiment was repeated six times using different
detector biases and run times. See subentries 2 - 7.
Fission fragments detected using xenon gas
scintillator tube; neutrons detected using NE-213
scintillator.
NE-213 neutron detector efficiency measured relative
to long counter response for neutrons from p-7Li
reaction below neutron energies of 4 MeV, and
calculated by computer above 4 MeV. TOF energy scale
determined using carbon scattering resonances.
Data corrected for random background, pulse
shape discriminator efficiency, room scatter and
pulse overlap.
Maxwellian fit was done for 6 results.
Numerical data probably received from author
(compiler's remark, Sept. 2011)
Approved by author 1976/9/1.
Converted to REACTION formalism
Converted to new date formats, lower case.
BIB update.
Subentry 10614.008 corrected.
OS. BIB changes, data units in
subentries 2-7 changed
Data given is actually the log(N(E)/sqrt(E)) where
N(E) is proportional to count rate per unit energy at
E.
Data errors given are statistical errors.
Energy errors due only to uncertainty in
prompt gamma peak location.
